rationale.R1.1: The abstract frames DISAMBIG-FiD as 'the first end-to-end generative model' with 'a single seq2seq backbone,' but the architecture section describes a conventional pipeline of three separately trained modules. The contribution claim and gap statement must be reconciled with what the system actually is.
rationale.R1.2: Two load-bearing internal contradictions are present: (1) the introduction asserts 'all three steps in one forward pass' while the architecture section states components are 'trained sequentially, each with its own objective, and combined at inference time'; (2) the results attribute gains to a 'supervision-free approach' where the model 'never sees gold AmbigNQ disambiguations,' directly contradicting the methods section which states 'the rewriter is supervised on AmbigNQ disambiguated questions.' These contradictions invalidate the central claims if unresolved.
rationale.R1.3: The 'supervision-free' framing in the results section is flatly contradicted by the training protocol two paragraphs earlier. Additionally, the reported F1 scores (48.1 F1_ans, +5.3 over SpanSeqGen) are asserted without a supporting table, ablation, or error analysis, leaving the claim inadequately grounded.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' centrally includes prose quality. The architecture walkthrough mixes high-level claims with implementation specifics without clear transitions; the results paragraph conflates credit attribution with empirical reporting; sentence-level tightening throughout is expected.
